layer_map.what: Rewrite bar geometry + treatment to reference values using existing tokens; add ancestor-scoped method-select background override
Presentational-only edits: visible Save/Share labels (+ aria-label drop), canSend-gated aria-hidden `<kbd>` ⌘↵ keycap, className/structure

Bind to existing `--radius`, `--accent`, `--accent-soft`, `--bg-elev`, `--text-faint`, `--border`, `--font-mono`, `--fs-*`; per-method colour stays on the `[data-mstyle='soft'] .method.{METHOD}` cascade
Add Playwright CT computed-style EXACT-equality fidelity asserts + thresholded screenshot diff; keep existing behaviour/unit suites green

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: A single ancestor-scoped rule `.request-bar .request-bar__method.method` (specificity 0,3,0) sets the flat elevated treatment — `background: var(--bg-elev)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `border-radius: var(--radius)`, sizing, mono 11.5px/700 — and declares **no `color`**, so per-method text colour falls through to `[data-mstyle='soft'] .method.{METHOD}` (0,3,0).
key_design_decisions.why: The per-method **background tint AND colour** both live in `[data-mstyle='soft'] .method.{METHOD}` at standard specificity **(0,3,0)** (attribute + 2 classes). The existing 009 override `.request-bar__method.method` is only **(0,2,0)** — it wins sizing but **cannot** override that background. Adding the `.request-bar` ancestor reaches (0,3,0) parity, then **source order decides** — RequestBar.css imports after tokens.css, so the override wins. Flat bg is method-invariant so one rule suffices; colour is left silent so the per-method path still paints it. (Corrects the non-standard specificity notation in the existing 009 CSS comments. Overriding the per-method **background** is a NEW concern — 009 kept the tint — so not a flagged departure.)
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Per-method `(0,4,0)` enumeration `.request-bar .request-bar__method.method.{METHOD}` × 7 — DRY violation, bg is method-invariant. `!important` — brittle, fights the cascade. Reading/writing `data-mstyle` to neutralise — AC-3 forbids any non-Shell write.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Render the `<kbd>` element **only when `canSend`** (JSX conditional on the existing predicate); element absent from the DOM when disabled.
key_design_decisions.why: AC-10 requires the keycap to **not render** when Send is disabled — a DOM-presence contract, not a visibility one. Markup conditional makes presence the single source of truth, directly assertable. Present → AC-9; absent → AC-10; binary on the existing `canSend`, no new state.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Always-render + CSS `:disabled`-hide — element stays in the DOM, fails AC-10's "not render" and leaks to the a11y tree.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Visible `Save`/`Share` text supplies the accessible name; drop the now-redundant `aria-label`. Keep `<kbd>` `aria-hidden` so Send's accessible name stays `Send`.
key_design_decisions.why: AC-11 + §7 a11y constraint: avoid double-labelling; existing `getByRole(name)` queries (Send/Save/Share/GET) must still resolve, which the visible text guarantees.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Keep both visible text + aria-label — double-labelling, redundant accessible name.

key_design_decisions.why: AC-5 mandates an accent border **plus** an accent ring; `--accent-soft` already equals the reference's accent-14% mix — reuse over re-deriving.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: `outline`-based ring — reference renders a soft shadow ring; outline can't reproduce the 3px soft spread and clips differently.

file_impact.what_changes: Rewrite geometry/treatment to reference values (padding 12px 16px, gap 8px, 32px control height, `--radius` binding); URL focus accent border + `0 0 0 3px var(--accent-soft)` ring; Send `--accent` fill weight 600 + inset-highlight shadow; `.kbd` keycap styling; bordered ghost Save/Share; ancestor-scoped `(0,3,0)` method-select background override (no `color`). All rules under `.request-bar`.
Presentational only: visible `Save`/`Share` text labels beside icons (drop redundant `aria-label`); `canSend`-gated aria-hidden `<kbd>` ⌘↵ inside Send; className/structure edits. No logic change.

Add computed-style EXACT-equality fidelity assertions on enumerated `.request-bar` props (control height, radius, focus ring, method-select bg/border/colour, Send weight/shadow, keycap presence, labelled actions) + a thresholded screenshot diff (`toHaveScreenshot` maxDiffPixelRatio 0.01). Reuse the existing tokens.css + `data-mstyle='soft'` fixture scope + two-step dismiss gate.
Add a filled-state fixture (non-empty URL → enabled Send + keycap) for the new fidelity/keycap assertions if the existing fixtures don't cover it.
Keep green; add jsdom unit asserts for keycap DOM presence/absence (canSend), Save/Share accessible-name preservation, and aria-label removal.

doc_impact.what_changes: Optional one-line note that RequestBar matches the design reference; the existing RequestBar entry otherwise stands.

risks.risk: Specificity miss — override loses to per-method `(0,3,0)` background (soft tint bleeds through) OR per-method colour wrongly dropped
risks.mitigation: Override declares background/border/radius **only**, never colour; `(0,3,0)` ancestor scope + source order; CT EXACT computed-style assertion on **both** method-text colour and bg/border as the loud safety net.

risks.risk: Source-order fragility — if the Vite bundle ever emits component CSS before tokens.css, the `(0,3,0)` tie flips and the override silently loses
risks.mitigation: Ordering confirmed (tokens.css imported first; component CSS after); CT computed-style assert fails loudly on any cascade regression — no eyeballing.

risks.risk: Scope leak — a new rule not anchored under `.request-bar` regresses other `.method` consumers (TabBar)
risks.mitigation: Every new rule ancestor-scoped under `.request-bar`; AC-12 grep gate + 005 `.tabbar`-scoping precedent.
Ensure visible label text === prior accessible name before removal; keep `<kbd>` aria-hidden; run existing CT + unit suites as a regression gate.

risks.risk: CT fidelity flakiness — Radix dropdown dismiss arm-race + missing data-mstyle/tokens context + jsdom can't resolve computed/pseudo styles
risks.mitigation: Real-browser Playwright CT only; reuse fixture scope (import tokens.css + set `data-mstyle='soft'` on host) + two-step dismiss gate; clear `playwright/.cache`+`.vite`+`dist` when a build error names a clean file.

risks.risk: Q-1 reference values not yet runtime-confirmed
risks.mitigation: `design-auditor` runtime screenshot + computed-style diff against `design/reference.html` (filled state) at `/implement` before the fidelity ACs lock to the concrete numbers.

specialist_consultation.sub_question: Layer map, key design decisions, risks, constitution flags, minimal change, §6-respect for the localised RequestBar.css fidelity refactor
specialist_consultation.input_summary: Authored all tables; identified the method-select specificity tension and the (0,3,0) ancestor-scoped override that declares background/border/radius only so per-method colour falls through; no specialist relay needed

dependencies: None — no packages to install, no services to configure, no environment variables. Playwright CT, Vitest, zustand, Radix, and the Icon atom are all already in the project.
